summary: The Alfred codebase demonstrates strong consistency across naming, formatting, and import ordering. The primary inconsistency is the split registration style in bridge.rs (NativeFunc vs NativeClosure) and the lack of an integer-argument extraction helper alongside the existing string-extraction helpers. Test naming follows the given/when/then pattern almost everywhere. The functional-core/imperative-shell architecture is applied uniformly across all crates.

violations.detail: Handler parameter consistently abbreviated as 's: &mut EditorState' across all handler files — fine but differs from 'state' used in input.rs and app.rs

register_string_primitives and register_list_primitives use direct env.borrow_mut().define() calls instead of define_native_closure(), creating a different registration idiom from the other 60+ register_X functions

anomalies.detail: Module file exists on disk but is not declared in lib.rs — structural orphan
Single file serving as bridge for all Lisp primitives. Other crates split concerns into multiple modules (handlers/*.rs, facade.rs) but bridge.rs remains monolithic

import_consistency.notes: Import ordering is consistently: std > third-party (crossterm, ratatui, rust_lisp, ropey, tree-sitter) > alfred_core/alfred_lisp/alfred_syntax > crate::. This matches idiomatic Rust. No wildcard imports found in production code.

logging_uniformity.notes: No structured logging framework is used. Runtime messages are communicated via state.message = Some(format!(...)) rather than a logger. This is intentional for a TUI editor (no stdout) but means no trace/debug/info levels exist. Errors are surfaced to the user via the message line.

deviations.detail: HOME env var read directly via std::env::var("HOME") rather than through a config object — acceptable for a CLI tool but slightly inconsistent with the EditorState config approach

error_handling_consistency.notes: alfred-core defines AlfredError with typed variants (FileReadError, CommandNotFound, etc.) via thiserror. Lisp bridge uses RuntimeError{msg} from rust_lisp. app.rs surfaces errors as state.message strings. The dual-error-type pattern (AlfredError for core, RuntimeError for bridge) is internally consistent within each crate but creates a translation seam at the bridge boundary.

formatting_consistency.notes: Cargo workspace implies rustfmt defaults. All files use 4-space indentation. Trailing commas present on multi-line items. Line length is occasionally long in bridge.rs error messages but does not indicate formatter absence.

patterns_detected.detail: Pure functions in alfred-core (buffer, cursor, viewport); IO at boundaries in alfred-tui (run(), render_frame()). All handler functions are pure transformations returning Result<()>.

facade.rs provides a controlled API over EditorState for external crates. However alfred-tui/src/input.rs bypasses facade for many accesses, directly reading state.cursor, state.buffer, state.mode etc.
BUILTIN_COMMANDS static table in editor_state.rs decouples command names from handler functions. Applied consistently for all built-in commands.
All primitives follow: define_native_closure -> move closure capturing Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> -> borrow/borrow_mut -> return Ok(Value::NIL). Applied to 60+ primitives.
Test functions use given_X_when_Y_then_Z naming throughout alfred-core, alfred-lisp, alfred-tui and alfred-bin. Rare deviations exist for very short structural tests.

recommendations.title: Split bridge.rs into sub-modules by domain
recommendations.description: bridge.rs at 5669 lines is the hardest file to navigate. Split into bridge/buffer.rs, bridge/cursor.rs, bridge/hooks.rs, bridge/keymap.rs, bridge/theme.rs, bridge/panels.rs, bridge/strings.rs, bridge/lists.rs, bridge/filesystem.rs with a bridge/mod.rs that re-exports the public register_* functions. This would align bridge.rs with the pattern already established by handlers/*.rs.

recommendations.title: Add extract_int_arg and extract_bool_arg helpers to bridge.rs
recommendations.description: The integer-argument extraction match arm (Some(Value::Int(n)) / Some(other) / None) is duplicated ~20 times. Adding extract_int_arg(args, index, fn_name, param_name) and extract_bool_arg() following the existing extract_string_arg_at pattern would eliminate ~100 LOC of boilerplate and make argument validation uniform.

recommendations.title: Export or remove rainbow_csv.rs from alfred-core
recommendations.description: rainbow_csv.rs exists as a file but is not declared in lib.rs, making it an orphaned module. Either add 'pub mod rainbow_csv;' to lib.rs (if the module is intentionally part of the core API) or delete the file if it has been superseded by the Lisp plugin approach.

recommendations.title: Extract event dispatch logic from run() into a dedicated dispatcher
recommendations.description: The DeferredAction match block in run() (~100 lines) handles SaveBuffer, OpenFile, ExecCommand, Eval, and SaveAndQuit. Extracting this to execute_deferred_action(state_rc, action, runtime, highlighter) would reduce the run() event loop to its control-flow skeleton and improve testability.

recommendations.title: Define named constants for magic numbers in app.rs
recommendations.description: POLL_TIMEOUT_MS = 100 and DEFAULT_TERMINAL_SIZE = (80, 24) should be named constants rather than inline literals. This makes intent clear and centralises any future tuning.

recommendations.title: Unify EditorState into sub-structs to reduce field count
recommendations.description: EditorState's 35+ fields could be grouped into MacroState {recording, buffer, replaying, last_register}, JumpState {jump_list, jump_index, change_list, change_list_index}, SelectionState {selection_start, visual_line_mode}, and RendererState {line_styles, line_backgrounds, panels}. This would improve cohesion and reduce the blast radius of struct changes.
